nodes.en: Techniques for reconstructing or accessing lost or inaccessible data—including disk imaging, partition repair, logical file recovery from overwritten storage, and data forensics. Presupposes backup and storage redundancy failures; distinct from backup strategies by operating post-disaster when primary data is already unavailable.
nodes.cs: Techniky pro rekonstrukci nebo přístup k ztraceným či nedostupným datům—včetně vytváření diskových obrazů, opravy oddílů, logického obnovení souborů z přepsaného úložiště a forenzní analýzy. Předpokládá selhání zálohování a redundance úložišť; odlišuje se od zálohových strategií operováním po katastrofě kdy jsou primární data již nedostupná.
nodes.brief:
Data recovery covers techniques for reconstructing or accessing lost or inaccessible data, including disk imaging, partition repair, logical file recovery from overwritten storage, and data forensics.

The branch presupposes that [[11.04]] Backup Strategies and storage redundancy have already failed — it is the last line of defense rather than the first — and it is frequently complicated by [[11.03]] Encryption & Security, since a properly encrypted device with a lost key cannot be recovered by forensic means alone. Where the damage is at the encoding level rather than the physical-media level, remediation instead falls to [[11.01]] File Formats expertise — repairing a truncated or malformed file structure rather than imaging a failing disk.

The [NIST Guide to Integrating Forensic Techniques into Incident Response (SP 800-86)](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/86/final) documents standard forensic recovery method, and the [Library of Congress Digital Preservation program](https://www.loc.gov/preservation/digital/) addresses recovery planning within a broader preservation strategy.
